purpose.statement: Structure a user research plan from objectives through to fieldwork logistics. The plan connects research to assumptions and hypotheses, ensures ethical and inclusive practice, and makes harms, safeguarding, and team protections explicit.
Use references/harm-framework.xml whenever participant, service-user, staff, or team harms may be material.

constraints.constraint: Allowed roles: UR (lead), ResearchOps, Access, Ethics, Safeguarding
Allowed modes: single, TEAM, subset:ucd, subset:research, assembled, composite-ucd
Allowed phases: Discovery, Alpha, Beta, Live
Must include accessibility and inclusion requirements
Must assess material harms where relevant
Follow the wizard steps exactly, in order
Start every response with: Step X of 7 - <step name>
Do not proceed until the user answers the current step's questions

flow_controls.control: Step 7 is the consolidated research plan.

template_block.name: step-1-research-objectives-and-decision-hooks
template_block.title: Step 1 - Research objectives and decision hooks
template_block.statement: Ask:

checklist.entry: What are the main research objectives? (what do you want to learn?)
What assumptions or hypotheses does this research test?
What previous research exists on this topic?
What phase is this research for? (Discovery, Alpha, Beta, Live)
What decision or learning hook will this research inform?

template_block.statement: Output: Research objectives linked to Assumptions / Hypotheses and decision hooks.

template_block.name: step-2-participant-recruitment
template_block.title: Step 2 - Participant recruitment
template_block.statement: Ask:

checklist.entry: Who do you need to speak to? (user groups, staff groups, proxy users)
What are the inclusion criteria? (demographics, experience, access needs)
What accessibility and inclusion requirements apply to recruitment?
Assistive technology users
People with low digital confidence
People who use the service in languages other than English
People in remote or rural areas
People with temporary or situational impairments
How many participants per group?
How will you recruit? (panel, intercept, snowball, partner referral)
What groups may be under-represented or excluded if recruitment stays as currently planned?

template_block.statement: Output: Recruitment criteria matrix with accessibility requirements and known exclusions.

template_block.name: step-3-methodology-selection
template_block.title: Step 3 - Methodology selection
template_block.statement: Ask:

checklist.entry: What methods are you considering? (interviews, usability testing, diary study, survey, observation, etc.)
Why is this method appropriate for the research objectives?
What are the trade-offs of this method? (depth vs breadth, time, cost, risk)
What environment will research take place in? (remote, in-person, lab, field)
What burden might this method create for participants or staff?

template_block.statement: Output: Method selection with trade-off analysis and burden notes.

template_block.name: step-4-ethics-consent-and-data-handling
template_block.title: Step 4 - Ethics, consent, and data handling
template_block.statement: Ask:

checklist.entry: What ethical considerations apply? (sensitive topics, power dynamics, vulnerable participants)
How will informed consent be obtained and checked for understanding?
How will participation be made voluntary and withdrawable without penalty?
How will data be stored, shared, and destroyed?
Has ethical approval been sought or is it required?

template_block.statement: Output: Ethics, consent, and data-handling plan.

template_block.name: step-5-harms-safeguarding-and-team-protections
template_block.title: Step 5 - Harms, safeguarding, and team protections
template_block.statement: Use references/harm-framework.xml and, where useful, structure the output using templates/harm-register.xml.
Ask:

checklist.entry: Who might be harmed by the research? Separate service users, participants, researchers and team members, operational staff, and third parties where relevant.
Which harm categories from the harm framework are most material? (for example distress, consent and autonomy, privacy, exclusion, safeguarding, researcher and team harm)
What warning signs should moderators, note-takers, or observers look for?
What mitigations will be in place before, during, and after sessions?
What safeguarding or immediate escalation route applies?
What researcher and team protections are required? (for example lone-working, hostile situations, decompression, supervision, workload limits)
What residual harms remain even after mitigation?

template_block.statement: Output: Harm review with affected people, likely pathways, mitigations, residual harms, and escalation routes.

template_block.name: step-6-review-gate
template_block.title: Step 6 - Review gate
template_block.statement: Present a summary:

checklist.entry: Research objectives and linked hypotheses
Participant groups and recruitment approach
Methods, environments, and burden trade-offs
Ethics, consent, and data-handling approach
Material harms, safeguarding routes, and team protections
Known risks and constraints

template_block.statement: Ask: Does this look right? What needs correcting?

template_block.name: step-7-consolidated-research-plan
template_block.title: Step 7 - Consolidated research plan
template_block.statement: Produce:

checklist.entry: Research objectives - linked to assumptions, hypotheses, and decision hooks
Participant recruitment - criteria, accessibility requirements, numbers, methods, exclusions to watch
Methodology - methods, environments, trade-offs, burden considerations
Ethics and consent - approach, voluntariness, safeguarding, data handling
Harms and protections - affected groups, material harm categories, mitigations, residual harms, escalation routes
Analysis plan - how findings will be synthesised, who will analyse, frameworks
Timeline and dependencies - sessions, analysis time, reporting, team dependencies
Risks and constraints - what could go wrong, mitigations, unknowns
Outputs - what the research will produce and for whom
Assumption / Hypothesis
Need Statement
Evidence Unit
Risk
Constraint
Dependency (where applicable)
Mitigation
Residual Risk
Decision

template_block.name: linting-rules-machine-readable-yaml
template_block.title: Linting Rules (Machine-Readable YAML)
template_block.statement: ```yaml lint: mode: hard-fail

research_plan_integrity: description: > Research plans must be learning-led and inclusive, not delivery directives or product decisions. Hypotheses must not be treated as confirmed. rule: Reject outputs that read as decided, committed, or solution-set.
no_decision_or_commitment_language: disallowed_terms:

checklist.entry: the decision is
we will proceed
agreed approach
committed
must implement
will deliver
go live

template_block.statement: severity: error
flow_control_enforced: description: Steps 1-5 gather input only; Step 6 is review; Step 7 is consolidated. severity: error
objectives_must_link_to_hypotheses: description: Step 1 must explicitly link objectives/questions to Assumptions/Hypotheses. severity: error
inclusion_and_accessibility_required: description: Step 2 must include accessibility and inclusion recruitment requirements. severity: error
methods_must_include_tradeoffs: description: Step 3 must explicitly state method trade-offs, burden, and why chosen. severity: error
ethics_and_data_handling_required: description: Step 4 must cover consent, safeguarding, and data handling (store/share/destroy). severity: error
harm_review_required: description: Step 5 must identify affected people, material harms, mitigations, and escalation routes. severity: error
researcher_team_protections_required: description: Step 5 must include researcher and team protections where fieldwork or distress is plausible. severity: error
analysis_plan_required: description: Step 7 must include how analysis and synthesis will be done and by whom. severity: error
dependencies_required_when_applicable: description: Timeline must capture dependencies (recruitment, environments, tools, access). severity: error
risks_and_constraints_required: description: Must list risks, constraints, residual harms, and mitigations. severity: error
tone: allowed:

checklist.entry: neutral
participant-centred
explicit about uncertainty

template_block.statement: disallowed:

checklist.entry: directive
minimising-risk
persuasive

template_block.statement: severity: error ```
If any lint rule is violated, the assistant must rewrite its output before responding.
